_metadata.description: Deterministic ingredient-level interaction rules keyed by canonical database identity
_metadata.structure_note: One entry per ingredient (or per form-scoped variant). All conditions, drug classes, and dose thresholds for that ingredient are in one object.

condition_rules.mechanism: 7-Keto DHEA has hormonal activity concerns and is not established as safe during pregnancy.
condition_rules.action: Do not use during pregnancy unless directed by an obstetric specialist.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Hormonal activity and limited lactation safety data support avoiding use.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid during lactation due to insufficient safety evidence and endocrine-active profile.

condition_rules.mechanism: Ephedra has serious cardiovascular and neurologic risks and is FDA-banned as a supplement ingredient.
condition_rules.action: Do not use in pregnancy.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Adrenergic stimulation can antagonize blood-pressure control therapies.
drug_class_rules.action: Avoid use with antihypertensives.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Serious toxicity risk profile and regulatory prohibition for supplement use.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Ephedra-containing supplements should not be used.

condition_rules.mechanism: Synephrine is structurally similar to ephedrine, acts on adrenergic receptors, and significantly increases systolic and diastolic blood pressure, especially when combined with caffeine.
condition_rules.action: Avoid bitter orange / synephrine supplements. Commonly found in weight-loss and pre-workout products as an ephedra substitute.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Sympathomimetic effects directly counteract blood pressure-lowering therapy.
drug_class_rules.action: Avoid combination with antihypertensive medications.

condition_rules.mechanism: Adrenergic stimulation can raise blood pressure and heart rate.
condition_rules.action: Do not use in hypertension or cardiovascular risk settings.

condition_rules.mechanism: Stimulant profile and hemodynamic effects present maternal-fetal risk concerns.
condition_rules.action: Do not use during pregnancy.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Yohimbe can counter blood pressure-lowering therapy.
drug_class_rules.action: Avoid combination with antihypertensive medications.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Safety data are limited and stimulant effects are concerning.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid in pregnancy and lactation.

condition_rules.mechanism: Propylene glycol is metabolized to lactic acid via alcohol dehydrogenase and ~40-50% is renally excreted. Renal impairment reduces clearance, leading to accumulation and lactic acidosis.
condition_rules.action: Prefer products without propylene glycol when chronic kidney disease is present.

condition_rules.mechanism: Oral aloe (gel/latex/whole leaf extract) has safety concerns and may be unsafe in pregnancy.
condition_rules.action: Avoid oral aloe supplements during pregnancy.

condition_rules.mechanism: Aloe gel polysaccharides (acemannan) may stimulate insulin secretion and improve glucose uptake. Limited clinical evidence but preclinical data is suggestive.
condition_rules.action: Monitor glucose if combining oral aloe supplements with diabetes medications.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Possible additive glucose-lowering. Oral aloe may also alter drug absorption via laxative effects.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor glucose and GI symptoms.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: NCCIH indicates oral aloe may be unsafe in pregnancy and breastfeeding.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: This rule is intended for oral/systemic supplement use, not topical-only products.

condition_rules.mechanism: Enhances insulin-stimulated glucose uptake via GLUT4 translocation and reduces oxidative stress-mediated insulin resistance. May potentiate glucose-lowering effects of diabetes medications.
condition_rules.action: Monitor blood glucose when initiating ALA at doses >= 600 mg/day alongside diabetes medications.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Additive insulin-sensitizing effect may increase hypoglycemia risk, especially with insulin and sulfonylureas.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor glucose and adjust medication only under clinician supervision.

condition_rules.mechanism: Berberine activates AMPK, increases insulin sensitivity, and stimulates GLP-1 secretion. Produces glucose-lowering effects comparable to metformin. Additive hypoglycemia risk is clinically significant.
condition_rules.action: Do not combine with diabetes medications without physician oversight. Monitor blood glucose closely.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Additive glucose-lowering comparable to metformin. Also inhibits CYP3A4/CYP2D6, potentially increasing drug levels of co-administered medications.
drug_class_rules.action: Avoid unsupervised co-administration. Requires clinician-guided glucose monitoring and potential dose adjustment.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Berberine (as in goldenseal) has documented neonatal bilirubin/kernicterus risk concerns.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid berberine-containing supplements during pregnancy and lactation.

condition_rules.mechanism: Contains charantin, polypeptide-p (plant insulin analog), and vicine which stimulate beta cells and increase glucose utilization. RCT showed significant fasting glucose reduction.
condition_rules.action: Additive hypoglycemia risk with diabetes medications. Do not use as a substitute for prescribed medications. Monitor glucose.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Additive glucose-lowering effect, especially with sulfonylureas and insulin.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor for hypoglycemia and coordinate with prescriber.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Animal studies suggest abortifacient and embryotoxic effects.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid during pregnancy and lactation.

condition_rules.mechanism: Black cohosh may have uterotonic/hormonal effects and is not considered safe in pregnancy.
condition_rules.action: Avoid use during pregnancy unless explicitly directed by obstetric care team.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Pregnancy safety is uncertain and potential adverse effects are described in safety guidance.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid in pregnancy; lactation evidence is limited.

condition_rules.mechanism: Caffeine crosses the placenta and higher daily intake is associated with pregnancy risk concerns.
condition_rules.action: Keep supplemental caffeine low and account for all dietary caffeine sources.

condition_rules.mechanism: Caffeine causes acute blood pressure elevation (3-15 mmHg systolic) through adenosine receptor antagonism and sympathetic stimulation.
condition_rules.action: Limit caffeine-containing supplements. Monitor BP when starting caffeine supplements.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: High caffeine exposure can affect maternal-fetal and infant outcomes.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: This threshold applies to supplemental caffeine only; total daily caffeine from beverages/foods should be considered.

condition_rules.mechanism: Chromium can alter glucose metabolism and insulin sensitivity.
condition_rules.action: Use with glucose monitoring if on diabetes treatment.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Potential additive glucose-lowering effect.
drug_class_rules.action: Adjust medication only under clinician supervision.

condition_rules.mechanism: Cinnamon may lower blood glucose in some individuals.
condition_rules.action: Track glucose trends when adding supplemental cinnamon.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Potential additive glucose-lowering effect with diabetes medications.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor for hypoglycemia and coordinate with prescriber.

condition_rules.mechanism: Dong quai is traditionally associated with uterine and hormone-active effects; pregnancy safety is not established.
condition_rules.action: Avoid during pregnancy unless specifically directed by obstetric clinician.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Dong quai contains coumarin derivatives (osthole, ferulic acid) that inhibit platelet aggregation. Case report documented >2-fold INR elevation in warfarin-stabilized patient after 4 weeks of dong quai.
drug_class_rules.action: Use caution with anticoagulant therapy. Monitor INR closely.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Clinical pregnancy/lactation safety data are limited and precautionary avoidance is recommended.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Use only with clinician oversight during pregnancy or lactation.

condition_rules.mechanism: Galactomannan fiber delays carbohydrate absorption and 4-hydroxyisoleucine stimulates insulin secretion. Meta-analysis shows FBG reduction of 0.96 mmol/L.
condition_rules.action: Additive blood sugar lowering with diabetes medications. Separate dosing from oral medications by 2 hours (fiber impairs drug absorption). Monitor glucose.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Additive glucose-lowering and fiber-mediated delay of oral medication absorption.
drug_class_rules.action: Separate dosing from oral hypoglycemics by 2 hours. Monitor for hypoglycemia.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Traditional concern for uterotonic effects. May also lower blood sugar in pregnant women on glucose management.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid during pregnancy.

condition_rules.mechanism: Tanacetum parthenium parthenolide inhibits platelet aggregation and has demonstrated uterotonic activity. Historical emmenagogue use is well-documented. Cochrane and ACOG recommend avoidance during pregnancy.
condition_rules.action: Do not use during pregnancy.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Parthenolide inhibits platelet aggregation via thromboxane synthase; additive bleeding risk with anticoagulant/antiplatelet therapy.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor for increased bleeding tendency.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Parthenolide crosses biological membranes and has uterotonic and anti-platelet effects. No safety data in lactation.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Feverfew is contraindicated in pregnancy per NCCIH and traditional classification as emmenagogue. Avoid during lactation due to insufficient safety data.

condition_rules.mechanism: Viscous soluble fiber delays gastric emptying and slows glucose absorption. Meta-analysis of 6 RCTs showed significant reductions in FBG (-1.08 mmol/L) and 2h postprandial glucose.
condition_rules.action: Separate from oral diabetes medications by at least 2 hours (fiber delays drug absorption). Take with adequate water.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Fiber delays absorption of co-administered oral medications including metformin and sulfonylureas.
drug_class_rules.action: Separate glucomannan from oral diabetes medications by at least 2 hours.

condition_rules.mechanism: Garlic can reduce platelet aggregation and increase bleeding tendency around procedures.
condition_rules.action: Stop before elective surgery unless clinician directs otherwise.

condition_rules.mechanism: Garlic lowers blood pressure via hydrogen sulfide-mediated vasodilation and ACE inhibition (8-10 mmHg systolic). Additive effect with antihypertensives may cause symptomatic hypotension.
condition_rules.action: Inform prescriber if taking garlic supplements alongside BP medications. Monitor for dizziness or orthostatic hypotension.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Potential additive anticoagulant/antiplatelet effects.
drug_class_rules.action: Use only with clinician monitoring for bleeding signs.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Potential additive platelet inhibition.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor for bruising or bleeding.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Additive blood pressure lowering may cause symptomatic hypotension.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor BP when combining garlic supplements with antihypertensive medications.

condition_rules.mechanism: Ginger may influence platelet function at supplemental doses.
condition_rules.action: Discuss use before surgery.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Potential additive bleeding risk with anticoagulants.
drug_class_rules.action: Use with monitoring and clinician input.

condition_rules.mechanism: Ginkgo biloba increases hepatic CYP450 activity, accelerating metabolism of calcium channel blockers (nicardipine studied) and reducing their antihypertensive effect.
condition_rules.action: Inform prescriber. Blood pressure may be less well-controlled. Dose adjustment may be needed.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Increases metabolism of calcium channel blockers, reducing their plasma levels and antihypertensive effect.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor BP control when combining with calcium channel blockers.

condition_rules.mechanism: NCCIH reports some evidence of pregnancy safety concerns and expert caution for use.
condition_rules.action: Avoid routine use during pregnancy unless clinician-directed.

condition_rules.mechanism: Ginsenosides improve insulin sensitivity and increase pancreatic beta-cell insulin secretion. RCTs show significant postprandial glucose reduction.
condition_rules.action: Monitor glucose levels when combining with diabetes medications.

condition_rules.mechanism: Ginseng may affect platelet aggregation and coagulation parameters. Case reports of reduced warfarin INR documented.
condition_rules.action: Stop ginseng at least 7 days before elective surgery. Inform surgeon.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Mild additive glucose-lowering effect.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor for hypoglycemia symptoms.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Case reports documented reduced warfarin INR with concurrent ginseng use, possibly via CYP enzyme induction.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor INR closely if combining with warfarin. Inform prescriber.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Pregnancy/lactation safety data are limited and concerns are noted in safety reviews.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Prefer avoidance unless obstetric team approves.

condition_rules.mechanism: Goldenseal (berberine-containing) safety concern includes newborn bilirubin/kernicterus risk and should not be used in pregnancy.
condition_rules.action: Avoid goldenseal during pregnancy.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: NCCIH safety summary advises against use in pregnancy and breastfeeding.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid use in pregnancy and breastfeeding.

condition_rules.mechanism: Green tea catechins (EGCG) inhibit intestinal OATP transporters, reducing oral bioavailability of nadolol and potentially other beta-blockers, diminishing BP control.
condition_rules.action: Avoid taking green tea extract supplements close in time to beta-blockers. Separate doses by several hours.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: EGCG inhibits OATP transporters, reducing beta-blocker absorption and potentially compromising BP control.
drug_class_rules.action: Separate green tea extract from beta-blocker dosing by at least 2-3 hours.

condition_rules.mechanism: Gymnemic acids reduce intestinal glucose absorption and may stimulate beta-cell regeneration and insulin secretion. Clinical studies show FBG and HbA1c reductions.
condition_rules.action: Additive hypoglycemia risk with oral hypoglycemics and insulin. Dose adjustments may be necessary. Monitor glucose.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Additive glucose-lowering effect via multiple mechanisms.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor blood glucose closely when combining with diabetes medications.

condition_rules.mechanism: Hawthorn extract has mild antihypertensive properties via endothelium-dependent vasodilation and mild ACE-inhibitory activity.
condition_rules.action: Inform prescriber. Additive BP lowering may occur with antihypertensives.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Additive blood pressure lowering from mild ACE-inhibitory and vasodilatory activity.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor BP regularly during concurrent use.

condition_rules.mechanism: Iodine is essential in pregnancy, but excessive intake can adversely affect maternal/fetal thyroid function.
condition_rules.action: Keep total iodine intake within guideline ranges; avoid excess supplementation.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: High iodine intake can alter thyroid physiology and therapy response.
drug_class_rules.action: Coordinate iodine supplements with thyroid medication management.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Both deficiency and excess can be harmful; UL and recommended intake should guide use.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Threshold escalates only on excess; baseline reflects required-but-dose-sensitive nutrient status.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Iron chelates with methyldopa and certain ACE inhibitors (captopril) in the GI tract, reducing drug absorption and bioavailability.
drug_class_rules.action: Separate iron supplement from methyldopa or captopril by at least 2 hours.

condition_rules.mechanism: L-arginine increases nitric oxide production, causing vasodilation and BP reduction (5-7 mmHg systolic). Additive effect with antihypertensives risks excessive hypotension.
condition_rules.action: Use with caution alongside antihypertensives. Start low and monitor BP.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Additive vasodilatory effect may enhance hypotensive action beyond intended levels.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor for dizziness or lightheadedness. Discuss with prescriber.

condition_rules.mechanism: Glycyrrhizin can increase sodium retention and blood pressure.
condition_rules.action: Avoid in uncontrolled blood pressure unless deglycyrrhizinated form is confirmed.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: May blunt blood pressure control from antihypertensive therapy.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor blood pressure and review supplement use with clinician.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Glycyrrhizin inhibits placental 11-beta-HSD2, allowing excess maternal cortisol to reach the fetus. Finnish cohort studies documented >2-fold preterm delivery risk and neurodevelopmental harm (IQ reduction, ADHD risk) with heavy glycyrrhizin exposure.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid during pregnancy. Even confectionery licorice at sustained intake poses risk.

condition_rules.mechanism: Magnesium acts as a natural calcium channel blocker, producing modest BP reductions (2-4 mmHg systolic). Additive effect with pharmaceutical calcium channel blockers may enhance hypotensive action.
condition_rules.action: Generally safe at recommended doses. Inform prescriber if supplementing alongside calcium channel blockers. Monitor for dizziness.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Additive blood pressure lowering from natural calcium channel blocking activity.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor BP when supplementing alongside antihypertensives.

condition_rules.mechanism: Potassium supplements combined with ACE inhibitors, ARBs, or potassium-sparing diuretics can cause life-threatening hyperkalemia.
condition_rules.action: Do not take potassium supplements without physician approval if on ACE inhibitors, ARBs, or potassium-sparing diuretics.

condition_rules.mechanism: Impaired renal potassium excretion greatly increases hyperkalemia risk with supplemental potassium.
condition_rules.action: Avoid potassium supplements with kidney disease unless directed by nephrologist.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: ACE inhibitors and ARBs reduce aldosterone, decreasing renal potassium excretion. Adding exogenous potassium raises serum K+ to dangerous levels.
drug_class_rules.action: Requires serum potassium monitoring. Do not supplement without clinician supervision.

condition_rules.mechanism: Soluble viscous fiber delays gastric emptying and slows carbohydrate absorption, reducing postprandial glucose spikes. RCT showed 11% reduction in all-day postprandial glucose.
condition_rules.action: Separate dosing from oral diabetes medications by at least 2 hours (fiber delays drug absorption). Monitor glucose.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Fiber delays absorption of co-administered oral medications including metformin and sulfonylureas.
drug_class_rules.action: Separate psyllium from oral diabetes medications by at least 2 hours.

condition_rules.mechanism: NCCIH notes potential increased birth-defect risk and major drug interaction potential.
condition_rules.action: Avoid St. John’s wort during pregnancy unless specialist-supervised.

condition_rules.mechanism: St. John's Wort induces CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, reducing plasma levels of calcium channel blockers (nifedipine, amlodipine, verapamil) and potentially other antihypertensives.
condition_rules.action: Avoid if taking calcium channel blockers or other CYP3A4-metabolized BP medications.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Potent CYP3A4/P-gp induction reduces plasma levels of calcium channel blockers and other CYP3A4-metabolized antihypertensives, compromising BP control.
drug_class_rules.action: Avoid combination. If SJW is discontinued, BP medications may suddenly become more potent, risking hypotension.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: CYP3A4/P-gp induction reduces warfarin plasma levels, decreasing anticoagulant effect. Documented INR reductions requiring warfarin dose increases.
drug_class_rules.action: Avoid combination with warfarin and other anticoagulants.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: CYP3A4 induction dramatically reduces cyclosporine, tacrolimus, and other immunosuppressant levels. Documented transplant rejections attributed to SJW co-administration.
drug_class_rules.action: Do not use with immunosuppressants. Risk of organ transplant rejection.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: CYP3A4 induction reduces plasma levels of simvastatin, atorvastatin, and other CYP3A4-metabolized statins.
drug_class_rules.action: Avoid with CYP3A4-metabolized statins or monitor lipid levels closely.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: CYP3A4 induction reduces levels of benzodiazepines (alprazolam, midazolam). Also risk of serotonin syndrome if combined with SSRIs/SNRIs.
drug_class_rules.action: Avoid unsupervised combination with sedatives or antidepressants.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Potential fetal risk and broad CYP/P-gp interactions.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Use in pregnancy should be avoided outside specialist care.

condition_rules.mechanism: Vanadium is insulin-mimetic: activates insulin receptor substrate, inhibits PTP-1B, and reduces hepatic glucose production. Clinical studies showed FBG and HbA1c reductions at 150 mg/day.
condition_rules.action: Additive hypoglycemia risk with insulin and oral hypoglycemics. GI side effects common. Use only under medical supervision.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Insulin-mimetic activity creates additive hypoglycemia risk. Narrow therapeutic window.
drug_class_rules.action: Use only under clinician supervision with glucose monitoring.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Limited safety data and insulin-mimetic activity present risk concerns.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid during pregnancy and lactation.

form_scope: vitamin A from cod liver oil

condition_rules.mechanism: Excess preformed vitamin A intake in pregnancy is associated with teratogenic risk.
condition_rules.action: Avoid high-dose preformed vitamin A in pregnancy unless clinician-directed.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Dose and form dependent risk is primarily tied to preformed retinoids.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Applies to preformed vitamin A forms; carotenoids are not handled by this threshold rule.

condition_rules.mechanism: At pharmacologic doses (>=1g/day), niacin increases hepatic glucose output and decreases insulin sensitivity. FPG increases of 4-5% and HbA1c increases up to 0.3% documented.
condition_rules.action: Can worsen glycemic control. Monitor glucose after initiating niacin or increasing dose. Low-dose niacin in multivitamins is not a concern.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Niacin can raise blood glucose, potentially requiring adjustment of diabetes medication doses.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor glucose closely. Diabetes medication dose adjustment may be needed.

condition_rules.mechanism: Vitamin C is essential, but excessive supplemental intake above UL is not recommended in pregnancy.
condition_rules.action: Keep vitamin C intake within guideline ranges; avoid high-dose supplementation without clinical need.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Dose-dependent tolerability; excessive daily intake can increase adverse-effect risk.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Threshold escalates only when intake exceeds adult UL.

condition_rules.mechanism: Vitamin E is essential, but excessive supplemental intake above established UL is not recommended in pregnancy.
condition_rules.action: Keep total daily vitamin E intake within guideline ranges and avoid megadosing.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Dose-dependent risk profile; high intake can increase adverse-effect risk.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Threshold escalates only when intake exceeds adult UL.

condition_rules.mechanism: Vitamin K intake changes coagulation balance around perioperative anticoagulation management.
condition_rules.action: Coordinate supplement use with surgeon and stop unless explicitly instructed to continue.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Vitamin K can oppose anticoagulant therapy and alter INR control.
drug_class_rules.action: Do not start/stop without clinician-guided anticoagulation monitoring.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Longer half-life menaquinone forms may have prolonged interaction potential with anticoagulants.
drug_class_rules.action: Escalate anticoagulant interaction warning for MK-7 forms.

condition_rules.mechanism: Yohimbe has stimulant and hemodynamic effects with concerning maternal-fetal risk profile.
condition_rules.action: Do not use during pregnancy.

condition_rules.mechanism: Adrenergic stimulation may increase blood pressure and cardiovascular risk.
condition_rules.action: Do not use in hypertension or cardiovascular risk settings.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Yohimbe can counter blood pressure-lowering effects.
drug_class_rules.action: Avoid co-use with antihypertensive medications.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Safety data are limited and stimulant effects raise risk concerns.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid in pregnancy and lactation.

condition_rules.mechanism: Concentrated ginger extracts may affect platelet function.
condition_rules.action: Review pre-op supplement use.

drug_class_rules.mechanism: Potential additive bleeding tendency.
drug_class_rules.action: Monitor with clinician guidance.

condition_rules.mechanism: Caulophyllum thalictroides contains caulosaponin and methylcytisine, which stimulate uterine contractions. Case reports document neonatal stroke, heart failure, and multi-organ injury following maternal use near term.
condition_rules.action: Do not use during pregnancy.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Uterotonic glycosides (caulosaponin) and nicotinic alkaloids (methylcytisine) pose direct fetal risk including cardiac toxicity and CNS injury.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Multiple case reports of serious neonatal adverse events. ACOG and NCCIH advise against use in pregnancy.

condition_rules.mechanism: Artemisia vulgaris contains thujone and other volatile terpenoids with emmenagogue and abortifacient activity. Animal studies demonstrate uterotonic effects.
condition_rules.action: Do not use during pregnancy.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Thujone-containing Artemisia species have documented emmenagogue activity. Traditional abortifacient use is well-described historically.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Avoid all Artemisia vulgaris preparations during pregnancy and lactation.

condition_rules.mechanism: Ruta graveolens contains furanoquinoline alkaloids (dictamnine, skimmianine) and furanocoumarins with established abortifacient activity. Animal studies confirm embryotoxic and teratogenic effects at moderate doses.
condition_rules.action: Do not use during pregnancy.

pregnancy_lactation.mechanism: Ruta alkaloids cause uterine smooth muscle contraction and have embryotoxic effects demonstrated in animal models.
pregnancy_lactation.notes: Rue is a well-documented traditional abortifacient. Avoid all preparations during pregnancy and lactation.
